rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELWOOD MARCH HAYNES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement; And To Modify Court?S 2007 Order Regarding Unitrust Conversion
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GE MING LUI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. No later than noon on Monday, August 14, 2022, Counsel shall file a supplement to the petition addressing the following issues: please identify Jannie by relationship to the decedent, any of the heirs, or counsel; please state her qualifications and experience with probate administrations; please state whether she can be fully bonded, and if not, counsel should be prepared to address petitioning to appoint a private professional fiduciary or other qualified person who can be fully bonded, as the court is not inclined to appoint a new administrator who requires the funds to be placed in 7 different blocked accounts. Counsel may also be asked to confirm the estate is ready to be closed, as it now holds only cash. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 11th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GE MING LUI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Administrator
rulings.Rulings: this petition taken off calendar by the judicial officer after review, as no statutory authority was cited and the court is not aware of any statute authorizing or requiring such a petition. the resignation of the administrator was effective as of the date on that resignation, 11/1/21. The former administrator will be ordered to file her second and final accounting when a new administrator is appointed. the court is not prepared to address the additional requests for relief at this time on this record.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RUTHIE OLAFSEN OIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authority To Execute And Fund Trust And Execute Pour Over Will By Substituted Judgment, Peition To Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; And Petition To Set Trustee'S Bond
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Assets on Exhibit A. In the second supplement 2 Exhibit A's are proposed. The difference between the two is one Exhibit A says #7, "All right title and interest in the Settlors' personal property and real property to the extent those assets do not have a beneficiary designation. The other Exhibit A does not include this language. 3) Asset on Exhibit A. The second supplement 2 Exhibit A's list EQ Shareholder Services Account #3063 (this account number is in the process of being changed as the account is being transferred to the conservatorship). Unclear if this is an IRA or if it's being transferred to the conservatorship why it is listed on the trust Exhibit A. 4) Judicial officer will determine at hearing whether to approve the new language in V(I) that states period payments of compensation on account, subject to the requirements of Probate Code section 2643 and rule 7.755 are allowed. 5) Judicial officer will determine at hearing whether to approve the new language in VI(F) that states the Trustee may pay himself periodic payments of compensation on account, subject to the requirements of Probate Code section 2643 and California Rule of Court 7.755. 6) The judicial officer issued a note regarding section VI(E) contemplates that the trust may continue well past the settlor's death. Consider revising this section to provide a course of action in the event of a minor beneficiary that does not depend on the continued existence of this trust. Counsel alleges there are two options distribute to a guardianship or into a CUTMA account. CUTMA option was picked because it is more cost effective.
